refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes
Batch update process at fixed intervals
Event-driven update triggered by external or internal signals
Parallel processing of update tasks to meet latency requirements
Prioritization and filtering of update data to reduce processing load
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on updating only changed parts of the worksite model, which logically decouples update processing from full model rebuilds, supporting independence and meeting latency constraints.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that changes can be detected reliably and incrementally
Risk that incremental updates may become complex if changes are interdependent
Assumes sufficient computational resources to process incremental updates within latency targets
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the update triggering mechanism and data filtering strategies to further clarify independence and support latency requirements.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing model updates and integration with other system components.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data inputs that may trigger model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated model tasks to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication for update propagation and synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental model update mechanism is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Low containment since Vehicle focuses on physical control; model update logic would diffuse heavily outside.
Partial containment possible but model update is not primarily a teleoperation function, causing moderate diffusion.
Poor containment; safety services are peripheral to model update mechanism, leading to high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental model update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for core system logic and model management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if update triggers require frequent cross-module communication.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution with too many responsibilities if other update-related functions are added without clear boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes' directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by focusing on incremental updates triggered by changes, which supports meeting the latency acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if update triggers require frequent cross-module communication.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment for the incremental model update mechanism with minimal diffusion (diffusion score 2). Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate. However, there is a risk of diffusion through interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) and ensuring the NGHE Solution module correctly processes telemetry inputs and sends timely updates via defined interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with the module boundary encompassing core system logic and model management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interface protocols and data contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to manage complexity and reduce diffusion risk.
Monitor and limit the scope of responsibilities within NGHE Solution to avoid overloading the module as update-related functions evolve.
Include performance monitoring within NGHE Solution to ensure compliance with the latency acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justification and feasible verification. The DP and IM selections directly address the FR with acceptable containment and manageable diffusion risks. Recommended revisions focus on interface definition and monitoring rather than fundamental decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear, well-contained module boundary is crucial, and current decomposition suffices to meet latency and safety requirements.

revise.why: To explicitly define interface protocols and data contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to manage complexity and reduce diffusion risk, and to include performance monitoring within NGHE Solution to ensure latency compliance.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism sufficiently meets the timeliness and safety requirements.
NGHE Solution module can be effectively monitored and controlled to prevent overload and diffusion.
Interface complexity can be managed through protocol definition without further decomposition.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity and diffusion with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Risk of module overload as update-related functions evolve.
evidence_needed: Performance monitoring data showing update latency p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
Verification of interface protocol adherence between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Assessment reports on module cohesion and diffusion over time.
